published.title: Do grades have absolute meaning? An experiment on majority judgment
published.authors: T. Delemazure, A. Baujard, R. Brunetti, S. Bouveret
published.abstract: Explores whether grade labels convey consistent meaning across voters in majority judgment voting, using an online experiment (N=1,955) on French presidential elections. Grade distributions are significantly affected by the scale used.

published.title: Land, wealth, and taxation
published.authors: R. Brunetti, C. Gaigné, F. Moizeau
published.abstract: Examines land's role in wealth inequality and develops an optimal tax approach combining land value taxation with lifetime wealth taxation.

published.title: Representative policymakers? A behavioural experiment with French politicians
published.abstract: A behavioural experiment (N=1,415) analysing the alignment between politicians' decisions and citizens' preferences in taxation–redistribution scenarios.

published.title: How people understand voting rules
published.authors: A. Baujard, R. Brunetti, I. Lebon, S. Marsilio
published.abstract: A lab experiment examining comprehension of evaluative voting and majority judgment, finding heterogeneous understanding of vote aggregation under majority judgment.

published.title: Credit market imperfections, urban land rents, and the Henry George theorem
published.authors: R. Brunetti, C. Gaigné, F. Moizeau
published.abstract: Analyses credit market effects on urban land pricing and derives optimal tax schedules under mortgage constraints.

working.title: Trickle-down economics, merit, and redistribution: an experiment with the poorest and richest US Americans
working.authors: G. Grimalda, M. Marino, R. Brunetti
working.abstract: An experimental study (N=2,346) investigating redistribution attitudes among extreme income groups, finding that philanthropic donations reduce redistribution demand among lower-income participants.

working.title: Municipalities' voting power and housing supply: evidence from French intermunicipal cooperation
working.abstract: Analyses how voting power shifts within local government councils affect housing density composition following municipal reform.

wip.title: Mediation, fairness, and efficiency in bargaining
wip.authors: with J. V. Ferreira, B. Tarroux

wip.title: United we fall? Reelection of mayors in merged municipalities
Merit, environmental externalities, and redistribution: an experiment
wip.authors: with F. Gonzalez, M. Marino, J. Mollerstrom

wip.title: An experiment on the number of candidates in approval voting
wip.authors: with A. Baujard, J. Lang, T. Delemazure
